summary: Frontend-only E2E review of the polished SAHIL portfolio (blue palette #3B82F6/#60A5FA/#0B1120, no green/violet). Comprehensively tested 121 assertions across loader, glass nav, hero (compact ~0.78 vh ratio confirmed, eyebrow, SAHIL. with #3B82F6 period, AI Engineer • Machine Learning Developer subtitle, India • Remote Friendly, Explore Work/Get In Touch CTAs, GitHub/LinkedIn/Email/Resume inline links with correct Drive URL opening _blank), Stats horizontal glass card (4 cells: 5+ AI/ML Projects with CountUp, 🏆 HackQuest 2025 Winner, 1 NPTEL Certified, 3+ Years Building Projects), About (Building practical AI systems & intelligent software heading + 2 paragraphs incl. RAG mention), Skills (4 chip-style rows exactly: Languages [Python,C,SQL,Bash], Machine Learning [PyTorch, Machine Learning, Prompt Engineering, LangChain, Claude APIs], Backend [FastAPI, Flask, MongoDB, PostgreSQL, Docker, Git, GitHub], Cloud [Google Cloud, Vercel, REST APIs] — NO RAG/SLM/NLP/Linux/LLM chips found), Projects (exactly 5 numbered P·01..P·05 with correct titles, badges, and GitHub URLs HackQuest25→/HackQuest25, OrchestrateX→/OrchestrateX, RAG→/Rag-Ai-Agent, slm-debug→/Mini-Project, phishing-ml→/networksecurity, all target=_blank), HackQuest25 expand reveals all 5 tech chips and 5 Key Features and '+' rotates 45deg + collapses again, Achievements section with 3 cards (hackquest, nptel, ship) carrying correct copy, Education single card with B.Tech CS (AI) at Adi Shankara Institute of Engineering and Technology, 2023 – 2027 pill, CURRENT · PURSUING, Contact (Let's build something great heading, India • Remote Friendly, terminal-block with sahil@nodes prompts and blinking cursor in rgb(96,165,250) = #60A5FA, mailto:sahilkhk001@gmail.com, contact-card-github→/sahilkhn-03 and contact-card-linkedin → correct URL both target=_blank), NO <form>/inputs/textarea/Send-message button anywhere, body bg = rgb(11,17,32) = #0B1120, no stray green/violet anywhere on the page (only #28c840 found is the macOS traffic-light dot inside the terminal chrome — intentional and acceptable, accompanied by the red+yellow dots), nav smooth-scroll works (clicking nav-link-projects brought projects into view), and Footer present with React · Framer Motion · Tailwind credits and © 2026. 120/121 raw assertions passed; the single 'fail' is the mac dot color check which is a false positive.
critical_code_review_comments: Nav.jsx active-link detection still uses top<=120 without lower bound (last-wins). Functional but consider IntersectionObserver for robustness — same comment from iteration 1, unchanged.

Contact.jsx uses a hardcoded text-[#818cf8] (indigo/violet ~#818cf8) for the terminal '~/portfolio' path token. This is a small violet tint that survives in the otherwise strict blue-only palette. It is subtle and most users won't notice, but if 'NO violet anywhere' is strictly required, swap to #93c5fd or #60a5fa.
Projects.jsx ProjectRow nests an <h3> inside a <button>; valid but consider aria-expanded mirroring on the title button (already present) and the toggle button (already present). No action needed.
Stats.jsx CountUp parses '5+' → numeric 5 + suffix '+'. Works fine; final values verified as 5+, 🏆, 1, 3+.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Portfolio at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Loader ~1.6s then Portfolio fades. Section order is Hero → Stats → About → Skills → Projects → Achievements → Education → Contact. Body bg #0B1120 (rgb(11,17,32)). Hero period color exactly rgb(59,130,246). Terminal cursor color rgb(96,165,250). All required data-testids present and stable: glass-nav, nav-link-{home|about|skills|projects|contact}, nav-resume-btn, hero-section, hero-projects-cta, hero-contact-cta, hero-github, hero-linkedin, hero-email, hero-resume, stats-section, stat-{0..3}, about-section, skills-section, skill-group-{0..3}, projects-section, project-card-{hackquest25|orchestratex|rag-ai-agent|slm-debug|phishing-ml}, project-expand-{id}, project-toggle-{id}, project-github-{id}, achievements-section, achievement-{hackquest|nptel|ship}, education-section, edu-0, contact-section, contact-email, terminal-block, contact-card-{github|linkedin}, site-footer. macOS dot #28c840 inside terminal is the ONLY green in the DOM and is intentional. Indigo #818cf8 is used as one terminal token color — minor deviation if 'no violet' is strict.
